description: Baseline manifest for principal-defect (cross-module / domain-grain) scenarios. Every principal-* scenario directory must be registered here. The selftest scripts/_principal_baseline_selftest.py enforces this contract mechanically. expected_baseline records the PRE-REGISTERED hypothesis; baseline_observed records the ACTUAL measured result (do not rewrite the hypothesis).

measurement.method: 6 parallel skill-equipped Critic reviews (current arm); rubric = references/architecture-rubric.md + method.md; neutral review prompt; verdict JSON graded by the host.
measurement.recall_flags: 3/3 caught (invariant-owner: Likely disqualifier; duplicated-rule: Serious deduction; process-owner: Likely disqualifier)
measurement.restraint_twins: 0/3 cleanly held — all 3 twins rejected, but each twin contained a REAL residual defect (scenario-quality bug), so this run does NOT measure restraint; twins must be rebuilt provably-clean-from-the-diff before restraint is measurable.
measurement.conclusion: The grain-ceiling hypothesis (loop blind to cross-module ownership defects) is FALSIFIED on recall: the existing rubric (Authority Map + state_management/domain_modeling/data_flow anchors) catches all three at baseline. The rubric also proved discerning enough to flag genuine residuals in the 'clean' twins. Implication: Phase-C grain-ceiling machinery (forces axis etc.) is NOT justified by this evidence for these defect classes. Open question: whether subtler principal defects with no clean smell-match would still be missed.

scenarios.observed_note: Rejected, blocks_95, Likely disqualifier, domain_modeling=5. Named multi-writer-state + weak-domain-model; cited 'invariants enforced at construction not convention' + 'one owner per mutable concern'.

Rejected — but the diff does not SHOW the two RosterStore methods delegating (convergence claimed only in prose) and the invariant check depends on a caller-supplied allRosters snapshot (soundness gap). Twin is not provably-clean-from-the-diff; rebuild needed.
Rejected, blocks_95, Serious deduction, domain_modeling=5. Caught the subtle point: constants centralized but predicate + one SQL bound not; demanded the 3->5 divergence test.

Rejected — OrderRepository re-encodes the rule STRUCTURE in SQL (is_active=1 AND subtotal>= AND prior_orders>=), not just the shared constants, so changing the rule still diverges silently. Real residual; twin is not clean. Rebuild needed (fetch-then-filter via isEligible, or policy emits the predicate).
Rejected, blocks_95, Likely disqualifier, data_flow=3. Named missing process owner + saga-in-presentation + temporal coupling; demanded a coordinator with compensation + partial-failure test.

Rejected — the 'clean' twin's compensation is WRONG: on confirm-failure after a captured charge it calls reservationService.release(seat:), producing pay-but-no-seat and reselling the paid seat. Real defect (inverted compensation); twin is not clean. Rebuild needed (retain hold / refund / idempotent retry).
